mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month. This inverse relationship holds consistently across market cycles.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds +0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positively correlated with rate levels.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5%, labor market slack triggers immediate profit margin compression and equity sell-offs within the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals robust aggregate demand, driving corporate earnings revisions and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of triggered cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive proxies.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of high inflation indicates supply-side bottlenecks that erode corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive when industrial production falls during high inflation periods, or the regime filter shows no predictive power relative to unconditional signals.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.5% in a single month signals aggressive monetary tightening, triggering immediate liquidity-driven equity declines within five trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in >60% of instances following a 0.5%+ rate hike, or the short-term reaction is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with stronger corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of monetary policy stance.
failure_condition: Rank IC is negative over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns show no significant correlation with quarterly GDP growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production during periods of stable inflation signals a sudden supply shock that disrupts manufacturing output, causing immediate negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of cases when industrial production drops sharply during stable inflation, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.
